manuscript_profile.title: The Lost World of MythOAmphibia / DOMINATUS I
manuscript_profile.genre: Mythic Eco-Fantasy / Multi-Species Civilizational Saga / Literary Eco-Horror
manuscript_profile.target_audience: Adult readers of literary fantasy, ecological speculative fiction, multi-species worldbuilding, body-horror-inflected mythic fiction, and transmedia/codex-ready mythos storytelling

txt: Mythic Eco-Fantasy / Multi-Species Civilizational Saga / Literary Eco-Horror
Literary Fantasy / Ecological Speculative Fiction / Mythic Multi-Species Saga
A poisoned frog civilization and a degrading human vessel collide as consciousness itself is distributed across bodies, objects, relics, and the dying Realm.
A single-species frog fantasy that erases the human degradation thread, object consciousness, and distributed Realm awareness.
Multi-species consciousness across all six domains
Object consciousness as memory and witness, not props
Author retains ownership of manuscript content

optional_public_strings: The truth of the manuscript is distributed; no single narrator owns truth.
Contamination-as-causality must drive pacing, not merely theme.
source_only_strings: MythOAmphibia is a multi-species civilizational eco-mythology where ecological harm, bodily transformation, object memory, amphibian survival, human violence, divine ambiguity, and realm-level consciousness form one governed narrative system.
The evaluator must not flatten the manuscript into frog fantasy, gross-out horror, environmental allegory, or animal adventure.
